label: Narrow-shell 3BR / baths CLUSTERED, SPLIT COLUMNS (zero-corridor) — the COMPACT CHAMPION of the narrow family: public spine runs down the west column (living, dining-kitchen, hall node, master), bedrooms + clustered bath block stack in the east column. lk style, no full corridor. Designed to solve 6.0 x 12.0 m (72 m²).

adjacencies.note: lk style — living (front-west, hosts entry) flows rear into the dining-kitchen; together they are the west public spine
the hall node sits at the rear end of the dining-kitchen — open mouth, no door (public spine continues into it)
br2 (front-east) doors west off the living room — the compact concession of this parti, same move as the squarish hall_core
br3 (mid-east) doors west into the hall node
master (rear-west) doors north into the hall node
common T&B (east, beside the node) doors west into the hall — guests reach it without crossing bedrooms
clustered bath block in the east column — common (front) and ensuite (rear) share one plumbing wall
ensuite sits east of master's rear position — interior door through the column wall
standards stack front-to-rear in the east column — wall only, no door

soft_proximities.note: kitchen (west column) and the bath block (east column) overlap at mid-depth — align them so both columns' wet points share one transverse under-slab run

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: This topology IS the compact floor of the narrow family — no fallback. At 6.0 x 12.0 m external (internal ~5.7 m) the columns split ~2.9/2.8: standards hold 2.8 m clear width at 9-9.5 m², master caps at 13 m², both baths compress toward hard mins. Below 5.7 m internal width the east-column bedrooms break 2.8 m clear — the parti fails, and so does every 1-storey 3BR narrow shell.

notes: 3BR narrow catalog topology #1 — SPLIT COLUMNS, the COMPACT CHAMPION and the
declared fallback of the whole narrow family. Natural floor band ~72-84 m².
Designed against the compact test: 10.0 x 16.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks all
sides) → 6.0 x 12.0 m shell (72 m²). Solves canonical 6.5 x 13.0 with room
to spare. Regime: single-detached, NO firewalls — both 2.0 m side yards are
code courts, so BOTH columns side-vent along the full depth. This parti is
impossible in the firewall/rowhouse regime.
Layout intent (canonical, west = left, front = bottom):
Rear:  [master        | ensuite      ]
..     [HALL node     | common T&B   ]
..     [dining-kitchen| br3          ]
..     [living        | br2          ]
Front: [  (entry)     |              ]
Program: no corridor at all. The west column is the public spine — living
(entry) flowing into dining-kitchen (lk), ending in a 1.0 m hall node, with
master at the rear-west corner. The east column stacks br2, br3, the common
T&B and the ensuite front-to-rear. Every circulation meter does double duty
as living space; that is what buys 3BR at 72 m².
Doors: br2 off the living room (bedroom_to_public — the one concession),
br3 + common + master off the hall node. Ensuite through the column wall
from master.
Plumbing: common+ensuite clustered on one wall (stack 1) at the rear-east;
kitchen sits diagonally across at mid-west, softly aligned so one transverse
run serves both. Dirty kitchen + service in the 2.0 m rear setback.
Ventilation: west rooms vent west yard, east rooms vent east yard, master +
ensuite also rear — every habitable room has an exterior wall (the
no-firewall premise makes the whole east column legal).
Carport policy (per brief, deferred): adding a carport later widens ONE side
setback 2.0 → 3.0 m (lot width +1.0 m). Topology unchanged — mirror so the
public column faces the carport side.
